task_groups.title: Write tests: project layout, build targets, and configuration

subtasks.title: Write tests for repository layout and Makefile build/test/lint targets
details: Create integration test file (e.g. internal/integration/layout_test.go) that verifies cmd/af-hub/ and cmd/afc/ directories exist and all non-entry-point packages reside under internal/.
Write integration tests that invoke `make build`, `make test`, and `make lint` as subprocesses and assert exit codes and output.
Write integration test that runs `bin/afc` with no arguments and asserts exit code 0 and no panic in stderr.
Write edge-case test TS-01-E1: inject a syntax error into cmd/af-hub/main.go, run `make build`, assert non-zero exit and non-empty stderr, and assert bin/af-hub was not (re)created.

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for configuration loading and validation
details: Create unit test file (e.g. internal/config/config_test.go) covering default application: port=8080, bind_address='0.0.0.0', database.path='./data/af-hub.db', logging.level='info'.
Write table-driven tests for validate_config: port out of range (99999), unknown log level ('verbose'), missing required OAuth fields — each must return a non-nil error naming the bad field.
Write unit test for directory auto-creation (os.MkdirAll): configure database.path to a non-existent nested path, call initialize_database, assert the directory is created.
Write unit test that external_url is loaded and accessible when set; validate_config returns nil.
Write integration tests for server startup failures: missing config.toml (TS-01-E2), invalid port (TS-01-E3), unwritable data directory (TS-01-E4) — assert non-zero exit and no port bound.

subtasks.title: Write unit and integration tests for database initialization
details: Create integration test file (e.g. internal/db/db_test.go) that opens a fresh in-memory or temp-file SQLite DB, calls initialization routine, then queries `PRAGMA journal_mode` and asserts 'wal'.
Write test that calls initialize_schema and checks sqlite_master for all five tables: users, workspaces, workspace_members, api_keys, admin_tokens.

Write constraint-enforcement tests: duplicate (provider, provider_id) in users, duplicate (user_id, workspace_id) in workspace_members, duplicate name/slug/url in workspaces — each must return a UNIQUE constraint error.
Write test that creates a user via the store layer and reads back id, created_at, updated_at — assert valid UUID and RFC 3339 string.
Write edge-case tests TS-01-E5 (read-only directory → non-nil error, nil db) and TS-01-E6 (conflicting schema → non-nil error mentioning 'users', connection closed).

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for store layer CRUD operations and error handling
details: Create unit test file (e.g. internal/store/store_test.go) with subtests for create/read/update/delete on all five entities; assert sentinel ErrNotFound on missing records.
Write test that calls store.create_user with no caller-supplied id or timestamps and asserts the returned struct has a valid UUID id and RFC 3339 created_at.
Write static-analysis test (or go/ast walk) that confirms no .go file outside internal/store/ imports database/sql directly or calls db.Query/db.Exec/db.QueryRow — covers TS-01-16 and TS-01-P3.
Write test that closes the DB then calls store operations; assert returned error is non-nil, contains operation/entity context, and no panic occurs (TS-01-E8).
Write test for UNIQUE constraint violation: insert same username twice, assert typed constraint-violation error returned (TS-01-E7).

subtasks.title: Write tests for admin bootstrap, token validation, and token rotation
details: Create test file (e.g. internal/bootstrap/bootstrap_test.go): test that run_admin_bootstrap with zero users creates admin user with expected fields (username, email, provider, provider_id, status).
Write test for generate_admin_token(): assert prefix 'af_admin_', total length 73, and last 64 chars are valid hex.
Write tests for hash persistence (TS-01-20), admin_token file write with mode 0600 (TS-01-21), and warn-level log emission of absolute path (TS-01-22).

Write integration tests for admin token validation: correct token → nil error (TS-01-23), absent token → non-nil error mentioning 'AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN' (TS-01-24), wrong token → non-nil error mentioning 'mismatch' (TS-01-25).
Write tests for --reset-admin-token: new hash differs from old, file updated with mode 0600, warn log emitted, AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN check skipped (TS-01-26, TS-01-27).

subtasks.title: Write tests for admin bootstrap and rotation edge cases, and property tests
details: Write edge-case test TS-01-E9: read-only config dir → admin_token file not created, users table empty after cleanup, non-zero exit.
Write edge-case test TS-01-E10: mock rand.Read to fail → generate_admin_token returns non-nil error, no file written, no entry in admin_tokens.
Write edge-case test TS-01-E11: users table non-empty but admin_tokens empty → validate_admin_token returns error mentioning 'corrupt'/'empty'.
Write edge-case test TS-01-E12: read-only config dir during rotation → admin_tokens hash unchanged, old token file preserved, non-zero exit.
Write property test TS-01-P1: generate 100 tokens, for each assert sha256(token) == stored token_hash.
Write property test TS-01-P2: bootstrap exactly once (users==0), not triggered when users>=1, idempotent on second call.

checks: All test files compile without errors: go build ./...
go vet ./... reports no issues on newly added test files
Test stubs/skeletons for TS-01-1 through TS-01-27 and TS-01-E1 through TS-01-E12 and TS-01-P1, TS-01-P2 are present and marked pending or skipped (not falsely passing)
No production implementation code introduced in this group — only test files

task_groups.title: Write tests: health probes, logging, shutdown, documentation, and property/smoke tests

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for health probe endpoints
details: Create integration test file (e.g. internal/integration/health_test.go) that spins up a test Echo server with health routes registered.
Write test TS-01-28: GET /healthz → HTTP 200, Content-Type application/json, body {"status":"ok"} — no DB interaction.
Write test TS-01-29: GET /readyz with healthy DB ping → HTTP 200, body {"status":"ready"}.
Write test TS-01-30: GET /readyz with forced DB failure → HTTP 503, body {"status":"not ready"}.
Write edge-case test TS-01-E13: mock DB ping to block indefinitely, assert response arrives within ~3s with HTTP 503 and warn log mentioning 'timeout' or 'deadline'.

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for structured JSON logging and graceful shutdown
details: Write unit test TS-01-31: call configure_logging('info'), capture output, emit a log message, assert valid JSON with 'msg' and 'level' fields.
Write integration test TS-01-32: make GET /healthz through a server with request logger middleware, assert log entry contains method, path, status, and numeric duration.
Write unit test TS-01-33: configure_logging('debug') → logrus.GetLevel() == DebugLevel; configure_logging('warn') → WarnLevel.
Write integration test TS-01-34: send SIGTERM to running server, assert new connections rejected and Echo.Shutdown called with 15s timeout.
Write integration test TS-01-35: SIGTERM with no in-flight requests → process exits with code 0.
Write integration test TS-01-36: SIGTERM with long-lived request held open beyond 15s → process force-closes, logs forced-shutdown warning, exits non-zero.

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for shutdown edge case, documentation presence, and remaining property tests
details: Write edge-case test TS-01-E14: mock db.Close() to block indefinitely, trigger graceful shutdown drain completion, assert process exits within ~10s and log mentions 'close' or 'timeout'.
Write unit tests TS-01-37/38/39: read README.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/configuration.md and assert required keywords/sections are present.
Write property test TS-01-P3 (static AST walk): no .go file outside internal/store/ calls db.Query/db.Exec/db.QueryRow or imports database/sql directly.
Write property test TS-01-P4: create one record in each of the five tables, read back all timestamp columns, assert all are valid RFC 3339.

Write property test TS-01-P5: for each failure scenario (missing config, invalid port, bad token, missing token), start server async, poll port 8080 for 5s, assert port never bound and process exits non-zero.
Write property tests TS-01-P6 and TS-01-P7: instrument SQL execution order to assert WAL pragma precedes all CREATE TABLE statements, and exactly five CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS calls are made.

subtasks.title: Write smoke test scaffolding for all five execution paths
details: Create smoke test file (e.g. internal/integration/smoke_test.go) with build tag 'smoke' or test helper that starts the real binary.

Write TS-01-SMOKE-1: first-boot path — start bin/af-hub with valid config.toml, no AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN; assert admin_token file created (mode 0600, format correct), DB rows exist, /healthz and /readyz return 200.
Write TS-01-SMOKE-2: subsequent boot — set AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN from prior admin_token file, start server, assert validation passes, health probes return 200, no new admin_token file written.
Write TS-01-SMOKE-3: rotation path — invoke bin/af-hub --reset-admin-token, assert new token hash differs, file updated with mode 0600, warn log emitted, /healthz returns 200.
Write TS-01-SMOKE-4: graceful shutdown — send SIGTERM to running server, assert exit code 0 within 16s, no panic in logs.
Write TS-01-SMOKE-5: readiness failure — force DB error, assert GET /readyz returns 503 within 3s with correct body.

checks: All newly added test files compile without errors: go build ./...
go vet ./... reports no issues on test files added in this group
Smoke test scaffolding (TS-01-SMOKE-1 through TS-01-SMOKE-5) compiles and tests are appropriately skipped until real binary exists
No production implementation code introduced in this group — only test files

task_groups.title: Implement Go project layout, Makefile, and CLI stub

subtasks.title: Scaffold repository directory structure and Go module
details: Initialize go.mod at repository root (module path e.g. github.com/agent-fox/af-hub, Go 1.22+).
Create directories: cmd/af-hub/, cmd/afc/, internal/.
Add placeholder main.go files in cmd/af-hub/ and cmd/afc/ so they compile.
Ensure all future internal packages will reside exclusively under internal/.

subtasks.title: Implement Makefile with build, test, and lint targets
details: Create Makefile at repository root.
Add `build` target: runs `go build -o bin/af-hub ./cmd/af-hub && go build -o bin/afc ./cmd/afc`; exits non-zero on compilation error.
Add `test` target: runs `go test ./... -count=1`.
Add `lint` target: runs `go vet ./...`.
Add `bin/` to .gitignore.

subtasks.title: Implement afc CLI stub binary
details: Implement cmd/afc/main.go as a minimal Go program that prints a placeholder message (e.g. 'afc: CLI not yet implemented') and calls os.Exit(0).
Do NOT use panic("not implemented") — must exit with code 0.
Ensure the binary compiles cleanly and is produced by `make build` at bin/afc.

task_groups.title: Implement TOML configuration loading and validation

subtasks.title: Define configuration structs and implement TOML loading with defaults
details: Create internal/config/config.go defining Config, ServerConfig, DatabaseConfig, LoggingConfig, and OAuthProviderConfig structs with TOML tags.
Implement LoadConfig(path string) (*Config, error) using github.com/BurntSushi/toml DecodeFile.
Apply defaults after decode: port=8080, bind_address='0.0.0.0', database.path='./data/af-hub.db', logging.level='info'.
Make external_url optional; expose it to callers as a string field with no validation beyond optionality.
Return a descriptive error if the file cannot be read (wraps OS error).

subtasks.title: Implement configuration validation
details: Implement ValidateConfig(cfg *Config) error in internal/config/config.go.
Validate [server] port is in range 1–65535; return error naming 'port' if invalid.
Validate [logging] level is one of trace/debug/info/warn/error/fatal/panic; return error naming 'level' if invalid.
Validate each [[auth.oauth]] entry has non-empty provider, client_id, and client_secret; return error naming the missing field.
Return a multi-error or first-encountered error with field name included in the message.

subtasks.title: Implement data directory auto-creation and server startup config integration
details: Implement EnsureDataDir(dbPath string) error in internal/config/config.go using os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dbPath), 0755).
In cmd/af-hub/main.go startup sequence: call LoadConfig → ValidateConfig → EnsureDataDir; on any error, call logrus.WithError(err).Fatal(...) and exit before binding any port.
Return OS error detail from EnsureDataDir so the caller can log it (wraps underlying error).
Ensure no port is bound before all three steps complete successfully.

task_groups.title: Implement SQLite database initialization with WAL mode

subtasks.title: Implement database open with pure-Go SQLite driver and WAL mode
details: Add modernc.org/sqlite to go.mod (blank import _ "modernc.org/sqlite" to register driver).
Create internal/db/db.go implementing OpenDatabase(path string) (*sql.DB, error).
After sql.Open, immediately execute `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL` and verify the returned value is 'wal'.
On failure to open or enable WAL: close any partial connection, return a wrapped error — never leave an open descriptor.
Return (*sql.DB, error) following Go conventions; no panics.

subtasks.title: Implement schema initialization with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for all five tables
details: Implement InitSchema(db *sql.DB) error in internal/db/db.go.

Execute exactly five CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements in order (after WAL): users, workspaces, workspace_members, api_keys, admin_tokens — with column definitions, constraints (UNIQUE, FK, composite PK) exactly as specified in the PRD.
On any CREATE TABLE failure: close db connection before returning error; include table name in error message.
Timestamps stored as TEXT RFC 3339; primary keys stored as UUID TEXT.

checks: go test ./internal/db/... -count=1 -v passes (TS-01-10, TS-01-11, TS-01-12, TS-01-13, TS-01-E5, TS-01-E6 green)
Property test TS-01-P6 confirms WAL pragma precedes all CREATE TABLE statements
Property test TS-01-P7 confirms exactly five CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS calls
go vet ./... exits with code 0
Requirements 01-REQ-3.1 through 01-REQ-3.4 and edge cases 01-REQ-3.E1, 01-REQ-3.E2 acceptance criteria met

subtasks.title: Define store types, sentinel errors, and Store struct
details: Create internal/store/store.go defining Go structs for User, Workspace, WorkspaceMember, APIKey, AdminToken.
Define sentinel error var ErrNotFound = errors.New("record not found") and var ErrConstraintViolation = errors.New("constraint violation").
Define Store struct holding *sql.DB; expose NewStore(db *sql.DB) *Store constructor.
Store is the ONLY package that imports database/sql or executes SQL — enforced by convention and tested by TS-01-P3.

subtasks.title: Implement CRUD for users and admin_tokens
details: Implement CreateUser, GetUserByID, GetUserByUsername, GetUserByProviderID, UpdateUser, DeleteUser on *Store.
Implement CreateAdminToken, GetAdminToken, UpdateAdminToken, DeleteAdminToken on *Store.
All IDs generated via uuid.New().String(); all timestamps via time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339).
Detect UNIQUE constraint violations from the sqlite driver and return ErrConstraintViolation (wrapped).
Wrap all other DB errors with fmt.Errorf("store: <operation> <entity>: %w", err); never panic or call os.Exit.

subtasks.title: Implement CRUD for workspaces, workspace_members, and api_keys
details: Implement CreateWorkspace, GetWorkspaceByID, GetWorkspaceBySlug, UpdateWorkspace, DeleteWorkspace on *Store.
Implement CreateWorkspaceMember, GetWorkspaceMember, ListWorkspaceMembers, DeleteWorkspaceMember on *Store.
Implement CreateAPIKey, GetAPIKeyByID, GetAPIKeyByKeyID, RevokeAPIKey, DeleteAPIKey on *Store.
Apply same UUID generation, RFC 3339 timestamp, error-wrapping, and ErrNotFound sentinel patterns as in 6.2.

task_groups.title: Implement admin bootstrap and token persistence

subtasks.title: Implement admin user creation and first-boot detection
details: Create internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go implementing IsFirstBoot(store *store.Store) (bool, error) — returns true when users table has zero rows.
Implement CreateAdminUser(store *store.Store) (*store.User, error) — creates user with username='admin', email='admin@localhost', provider='local', provider_id='admin', status='active'.
On any error creating the user, return the error; caller is responsible for cleanup.
In cmd/af-hub/main.go startup sequence: call IsFirstBoot; if true, call bootstrap flow.

subtasks.title: Implement admin token generation and hash persistence
details: Implement GenerateAdminToken() (string, error) using crypto/rand.Read to generate 32 random bytes, hex-encode to 64 chars, prepend 'af_admin_'.
Return error if crypto/rand fails; do not produce partial tokens.
Implement PersistAdminTokenHash(store *store.Store, plaintext string) error — computes sha256.Sum256, hex-encodes, calls store.CreateAdminToken or store.UpdateAdminToken.
Implement UpdateAdminTokenHash(store *store.Store, plaintext string) error for rotation — same hash logic, replaces existing row.

subtasks.title: Implement admin_token file write and bootstrap orchestration
details: Implement WriteAdminTokenFile(plaintext, configDir string) error using os.WriteFile with permission 0600; return OS error on failure.
Implement RunAdminBootstrap(store *store.Store, configDir string) error orchestrating: CreateAdminUser → GenerateAdminToken → PersistAdminTokenHash → WriteAdminTokenFile → logrus.Warn(absolutePath).
On WriteAdminTokenFile failure: remove any partial file with os.Remove (ignore error), delete the admin user record via store.DeleteUser, return error for fatal logging in main.
Use filepath.Abs to compute and log the absolute path of the admin_token file at warn level.

checks: go test ./internal/bootstrap/... -count=1 -v passes (TS-01-18 through TS-01-22, TS-01-E9, TS-01-E10 green)

Requirements 01-REQ-5.1 through 01-REQ-5.5 and edge cases 01-REQ-5.E1, 01-REQ-5.E2 acceptance criteria met

task_groups.title: Implement admin token validation and rotation

subtasks.title: Implement admin token validation on subsequent boots
details: Implement ValidateAdminToken(store *store.Store) error in internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go.
Read os.Getenv("AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN"); if empty, return descriptive error mentioning 'AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN'.
Fetch admin token record via store.GetAdminToken(); if not found or table empty, return error mentioning 'corrupt' or 'no token'.
Compute sha256.Sum256([]byte(env_token)), hex-encode; compare with stored token_hash; if mismatch, return error mentioning 'mismatch'.
Return nil only if all checks pass.

subtasks.title: Implement admin token rotation via --reset-admin-token flag
details: Parse --reset-admin-token flag in cmd/af-hub/main.go using flag package or os.Args inspection.
Implement RotateAdminToken(store *store.Store, configDir string) error: GenerateAdminToken → WriteAdminTokenFile (mode 0600) → UpdateAdminTokenHash → logrus.Warn(absolutePath).
On WriteAdminTokenFile failure: do NOT update admin_tokens (or rollback if already updated), return error; leave old token intact.
When --reset-admin-token is set, skip ValidateAdminToken call in startup sequence and proceed directly to HTTP server bind.

checks: go test ./internal/bootstrap/... -count=1 -v passes (TS-01-23 through TS-01-27, TS-01-E11, TS-01-E12 green)
go vet ./... exits with code 0
Requirements 01-REQ-6.1 through 01-REQ-7.2 and edge cases 01-REQ-6.E1, 01-REQ-7.E1 acceptance criteria met
No port binding occurs before ValidateAdminToken (or RotateAdminToken) completes — verified by TS-01-P5

task_groups.title: Implement health probe endpoints and Echo HTTP server setup

subtasks.title: Implement Echo HTTP server initialization and route registration
details: Create internal/server/server.go implementing NewServer(cfg *config.Config, db *sql.DB) *echo.Echo using echo.New().
Register GET /healthz handler returning echo.Context.JSON(200, map[string]string{"status":"ok"}) — no DB interaction.
Register GET /readyz handler: ping DB using db.PingContext with a 2-second timeout; on success return 200 {"status":"ready"}; on failure return 503 {"status":"not ready"}.
In cmd/af-hub/main.go call NewServer after all startup validation passes; start server with e.Start(addr).

subtasks.title: Implement readyz ping timeout and warn logging
details: In /readyz handler, use context.WithTimeout(req.Context(), 2*time.Second) for the db.PingContext call.
On deadline exceeded or any ping error: log at warn level with logrus.WithError(err).Warn("readyz: database ping failed"), return HTTP 503.
Ensure response is always sent within ~2 seconds even if the ping hangs indefinitely.

task_groups.title: Implement structured JSON logging and request logger middleware

subtasks.title: Implement logrus JSON logging setup
details: Create internal/logging/logging.go implementing ConfigureLogging(level string) error.
Call logrus.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{}) to set JSON as the sole output format.
Parse the level string using logrus.ParseLevel; call logrus.SetLevel with the result.
Return an error if the level string is invalid (should be caught by config validation, but defensive).
Call ConfigureLogging from cmd/af-hub/main.go before the HTTP server starts.

subtasks.title: Implement HTTP request logger middleware
details: Create internal/middleware/logger.go implementing RequestLoggerMiddleware() echo.MiddlewareFunc.
Record start time before calling next handler; after completion, compute duration in milliseconds.
Emit a logrus.WithFields entry containing: method, path, status (response code), duration (numeric ms).
Register the middleware on the Echo instance in NewServer before route registration.

checks: go test ./internal/logging/... ./internal/middleware/... -count=1 -v passes (TS-01-31, TS-01-32, TS-01-33 green)
go vet ./... exits with code 0
Requirements 01-REQ-9.1 through 01-REQ-9.3 acceptance criteria met
Log output for any request is valid JSON parseable by encoding/json

task_groups.title: Implement graceful shutdown with SIGTERM/SIGINT handling

subtasks.title: Implement signal handling and Echo graceful shutdown
details: In cmd/af-hub/main.go, start the Echo server in a goroutine and use signal.NotifyContext or signal.Notify to listen for syscall.SIGTERM and syscall.SIGINT.
On signal receipt, create a context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 15*time.Second) and call e.Shutdown(ctx).
If Shutdown returns nil (all connections drained): close DB, flush logs, exit with code 0.
If Shutdown returns context.DeadlineExceeded (timeout): log warn 'graceful shutdown timed out, forcing exit', close DB, exit with code 1.

subtasks.title: Implement database close timeout and clean process exit
details: After Echo shutdown completes (or times out), attempt db.Close() using a goroutine with a 5-second timeout.
If db.Close() does not return within 5 seconds: log error 'database close timed out' and proceed to exit.
Ensure the process always exits and never blocks indefinitely regardless of db.Close() behavior.
Use defer or explicit cleanup ordering to ensure log buffers are flushed before exit.

checks: go test ./... -count=1 passes (TS-01-34, TS-01-35, TS-01-36, TS-01-E14 green)
go vet ./... exits with code 0
Requirements 01-REQ-10.1 through 01-REQ-10.3 and edge case 01-REQ-10.E1 acceptance criteria met
TS-01-SMOKE-4 passes: SIGTERM on a running server → exit code 0 within 16 seconds

subtasks.title: Write README.md at project root
details: Create README.md at repository root.

Include sections: project overview (af-hub coordination hub for agent-fox), prerequisites (Go 1.22+), quickstart (make build, configure config.toml, run bin/af-hub, first boot admin token), project structure overview (cmd/, internal/), links to docs/architecture.md and docs/configuration.md.

subtasks.title: Write docs/architecture.md
details: Create docs/architecture.md.

Cover: two-binary design (af-hub server, afc CLI), internal/ package layout (config, db, store, bootstrap, server, middleware, logging), SQLite storage rationale (pure-Go, WAL mode, no CGo), config loading flow (LoadConfig → ValidateConfig → EnsureDataDir), request lifecycle (signal → Echo router → middleware → handler → response).

subtasks.title: Write docs/configuration.md
details: Create docs/configuration.md.

Document all config.toml fields: [server] port (int, 1–65535, default 8080), bind_address (string, default '0.0.0.0'), external_url (string, optional); [database] path (string, default './data/af-hub.db'); [logging] level (string, one of trace/debug/info/warn/error/fatal/panic, default 'info'); [[auth.oauth]] entries with provider/client_id/client_secret (required) and authorize_url/token_url/userinfo_url (optional).
Include environment variable section documenting AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN, its purpose, and when it is required.
Include validation rules and error messages for each field.

task_groups.title: Checkpoint: integrate all components into cmd/af-hub/main.go startup sequence

subtasks.title: Wire startup sequence: config → DB → schema → bootstrap/validation → HTTP server
details: Finalize cmd/af-hub/main.go startup sequence: parse flags (--reset-admin-token) → LoadConfig → ValidateConfig → ConfigureLogging → EnsureDataDir → OpenDatabase → InitSchema → (IsFirstBoot? RunAdminBootstrap : (resetFlag? RotateAdminToken : ValidateAdminToken)) → NewServer (register middleware + routes) → start Echo + signal handler.
Ensure each step's error causes logrus.Fatal before any port is bound.

TS-01-P5 confirms no port binding before validation passes in failure scenarios
make build, make test, make lint all exit with code 0

task_groups.title: Wiring verification: end-to-end integration, stub audit, and smoke tests

subtasks.title: Trace all five execution paths through production code
details: 01-PATH-1 (first boot): confirm main.go calls IsFirstBoot → RunAdminBootstrap → NewServer → e.Start in sequence with no stub returns.
01-PATH-2 (subsequent boot): confirm main.go calls ValidateAdminToken with real store, not a stub, and proceeds to e.Start only on nil error.
01-PATH-3 (token rotation): confirm --reset-admin-token flag causes RotateAdminToken to be called and ValidateAdminToken is skipped.
01-PATH-4 (graceful shutdown): confirm signal handler calls e.Shutdown(ctx) with 15s timeout and then db.Close() with 5s timeout.
01-PATH-5 (readyz DB failure): confirm /readyz handler calls db.PingContext with a real 2-second context, not an infinite timeout.

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation across startup sequence
details: Confirm errors from LoadConfig, ValidateConfig, EnsureDataDir, OpenDatabase, InitSchema are each checked and passed to logrus.Fatal in main.go — none are silently discarded.
Confirm errors from RunAdminBootstrap and ValidateAdminToken are checked in main.go and cause fatal exit.
Confirm /readyz handler uses the return value of db.PingContext to determine response status — never hardcodes 200.
Confirm Echo.Shutdown return value is inspected to decide exit code (0 vs non-zero).

subtasks.title: Audit codebase for stub and dead-code markers
details: Search entire codebase (excluding cmd/afc/main.go) for panic("not implemented"), // TODO, bare `return nil` in non-trivial code paths, and empty function bodies — none should exist in production code paths.
cmd/afc/main.go is the only permitted stub; confirm it exits with code 0 and does not panic.
Confirm all five store CRUD operations for all five entities have real SQL implementations (no placeholder returns).
Confirm RunAdminBootstrap, ValidateAdminToken, and RotateAdminToken have complete implementations, not early-return stubs.

subtasks.title: Run all smoke tests with real binary and assert full correctness properties
details: Execute TS-01-SMOKE-1: first boot produces admin_token file (mode 0600, format af_admin_<64 hex>), DB has admin user, /healthz → 200, /readyz → 200, warn log contains absolute path.
Execute TS-01-SMOKE-2: subsequent boot with correct AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN passes validation, health probes green, no new admin_token file.
Execute TS-01-SMOKE-3: --reset-admin-token produces new token hash, file mode 0600, warn log, /healthz → 200.
Execute TS-01-SMOKE-4: SIGTERM → exit code 0 within 16s, no panic in logs.
Execute TS-01-SMOKE-5: forced DB error → GET /readyz returns 503 within 3s with correct body and warn log.

subtasks.title: Verify all correctness properties hold end-to-end
details: TS-01-P1: confirm sha256(plaintext_token) == stored token_hash for 100 generated tokens.
TS-01-P2: confirm bootstrap runs exactly once when users==0 and is skipped when users>=1.
TS-01-P3: confirm no SQL calls exist outside internal/store/ via static-analysis test.
TS-01-P4: confirm all timestamp columns in all five tables contain valid RFC 3339 strings.
TS-01-P5: confirm no port is bound before validation succeeds in all four failure scenarios.
TS-01-P6 and TS-01-P7: confirm WAL pragma precedes CREATE TABLE and exactly five tables are created.

checks: go test ./... -count=1 exits with code 0 — all 39 functional tests, 7 property tests, 14 edge-case tests, and 5 smoke tests pass
go vet ./... exits with code 0
make build exits with code 0 and produces bin/af-hub and bin/afc
No panic("not implemented") or bare TODO stubs in any file except cmd/afc/main.go
All five correctness properties (01-PROP-1 through 01-PROP-6) verified by their respective property tests
All five execution paths (01-PATH-1 through 01-PATH-5) exercised by smoke tests with real components
All 15 error-handling entries (01-ERR-1 through 01-ERR-15) covered by unit or integration tests
